title: lab_db_storage â€” Database Storage & Index Lab
summary: A teaching database engine with two staged implementation tracks: Stage 1 builds a buffer pool manager (LRU/LRU-K replacers, RAII page guards, table/page handles), and Stage 2 builds a B+ tree index on top of those primitives (page structures, tree operations, index handle, and an index-scan executor).
instruction:
## Project

This is `lab_db_storage`, a small teaching RDBMS written in modern C++ (CMake build).
The full source tree lives at `/workspace/lab_db_storage/`. Your job is to complete
the storage layer, the B+Tree index, and the executors so that the unit and SQL
tests pass.

Implementation files of interest sit under
`/workspace/lab_db_storage/src/` (buffer pool, replacers, page guards, B+Tree
pages and index, table/index/page handles, executors). Public headers live in
`/workspace/lab_db_storage/src/include/` and shared types in
`/workspace/lab_db_storage/src/common/`.

Pre-built reference object files for unrelated components are provided under
`/workspace/lab_db_storage/lib-gold/` so partial work still links â€” do not depend
on them being present at evaluation time for the modules you are asked to
implement.

Do not read or modify anything under `/workspace/lab_db_storage/test/` or under
the task-level `tests/` directory; those are the grading harness.

## Modules to implement

Each `/workspace/requirements/<slug>.yaml` describes one module. Implement all
of them; the requirement files are the source of truth for per-module behavior:

- `lru_replacer` â€” classic LRU eviction for buffer frames.
- `lru_k_replacer` â€” LRU-K eviction using backward k-distance.
- `buffer_pool_manager` â€” frame allocation, fetch / unpin / delete, dirty
  write-back, integration with the replacer.
- `page_guard` â€” RAII `ReadPageGuard` / `WritePageGuard` with correct move
  semantics, dirty-flag handling, and automatic unpinning.
- `page_handle` â€” `NAryPageHandle` (row-store) and `PAXPageHandle`
  (column-store) slot read/write and chunk read on raw `Page*` memory.
- `table_handle` â€” record-level access (`GetRecord`, `InsertRecord`,
  `UpdateRecord`, `DeleteRecord`, `GetChunk`) backed by the buffer pool
  manager.
- `bptree_pages` â€” `BPTreePage`, `BPTreeLeafPage`, `BPTreeInternalPage`:
  headers, key/value accessors, `KeyIndex` / `LowerBound` / `UpperBound`,
  `Lookup`, `Insert` / `RemoveRecord`, and split/merge helpers
  (`MoveHalfTo`, `MoveAllTo`, `CopyNFrom`, `PopulateNewRoot`,
  `InsertNodeAfter`). `IsSafe` decides whether an op can proceed without
  violating occupancy bounds.
- `bptree_index` â€” `BPTreeIndex`: `NewPage` / `DeletePage` (free-list),
  `InitializeIndex`, `FindLeafPage` / `FindLeafPageForRange`, `Insert` with
  leaf splits and root promotion, `Delete` with redistribution or merge on
  underflow, `Search`, and `SearchRange` over linked leaf pages.
- `index_handle` â€” uniform `IndexHandle` facade over `BPTreeIndex` /
  `HashIndex` (`InsertRecord`, `DeleteRecord`, `UpdateRecord`,
  `SearchRange`, `CheckRecordExists`).
- `executor_idxscan` â€” index-range scan executor: drive `IndexHandle` to
  obtain matching RIDs, fetch the records via `TableHandle`, apply filters,
  emit tuples.

## Invariants worth knowing

- All data records (key / RID pairs) live only in leaf pages of the B+Tree;
  internal pages hold separator keys and child page ids only.
- Leaf pages are linked so that range scans can follow leaf-to-leaf
  pointers.
- Page headers track page id, parent page id, current size, max size, page
  type, and whether the page is the root; the helpers `IsLeaf`, `IsRoot`,
  `GetSize`, `SetSize`, `GetMaxSize`, `GetPageId`, `GetParentPageId` must
  stay consistent with those fields.
- Replacers must respect pin counts; pinned frames are never evictable.
- `WritePageGuard` treats the page as dirty on construction and whenever
  mutable data is handed out; `ReadPageGuard` never marks a page dirty.
- Use `FetchPageRead` / `FetchPageWrite` on the buffer pool manager when a
  component needs guarded access to a page.

The unit tests under `tests/` exercise the buffer pool, replacers, page
guards, and B+Tree behaviors described above. The harness will build and run
them â€” you do not run any tests yourself.

moduleDag.project: lab_db_storage â€” Database Storage & Index Lab
moduleDag.description: A teaching database engine with two staged implementation tracks: Stage 1 builds a buffer pool manager (LRU/LRU-K replacers, RAII page guards, table/page handles), and Stage 2 builds a B+ tree index on top of those primitives (page structures, tree operations, index handle, and an index-scan executor).

nodes.label: LRU Replacer
nodes.description: Implements the basic LRU eviction policy for buffer frames. Maintains a doubly-linked list and hash map; Victim() picks the least-recently-used evictable frame, Pin() marks a frame in-use, Unpin() marks it evictable again.

nodes.label: LRU-K Replacer
nodes.description: Implements the LRU-K eviction policy: tracks the last k access timestamps per frame and evicts the frame with the largest backward k-distance (infinite distance = fewer than k accesses, ties broken by earliest first-access time).

nodes.label: NAry / PAX Page Handle
nodes.description: Implements NAryPageHandle (row-store) and PAXPageHandle (column-store) slot read/write and chunk read. Operates directly on Page* memory with bitmap-controlled slot occupancy; independent of the buffer pool manager.

nodes.label: Buffer Pool Manager
nodes.description: Manages a fixed pool of frames mapping (file_id, page_id) pairs to in-memory pages. Supports FetchPage, UnpinPage, DeletePage, FlushPage using either LRUReplacer or LRUKReplacer; evicts dirty frames to disk via GetAvailableFrame / UpdateFrame.

nodes.label: RAII Page Guards
nodes.description: ReadPageGuard and WritePageGuard automatically call UnpinPage on the buffer pool manager when they go out of scope or are explicitly dropped. WritePageGuard marks pages dirty on construction and when GetMutableData() is called; move semantics invalidate the source guard.

nodes.label: Table Handle
nodes.description: Provides record-level access to a heap table: GetRecord, InsertRecord, UpdateRecord, DeleteRecord, GetChunk. Fetches pages from the buffer pool manager and wraps them in NAryPageHandle or PAXPageHandle to read and write slots.

nodes.label: B+Tree Page Structures
nodes.description: Implements BPTreePage (shared header: page id, parent id, size, max_size, IsSafe), BPTreeLeafPage (key/RID arrays, KeyIndex, LowerBound, UpperBound, Lookup, Insert, RemoveRecord, MoveHalfTo, MoveAllTo, CopyNFrom), and BPTreeInternalPage (key/child arrays, Lookup, PopulateNewRoot, InsertNodeAfter, MoveHalfTo, MoveAllTo, CopyNFrom). Structural helpers call FetchPageWrite (returning WritePageGuard) to update child parent pointers.

nodes.label: B+Tree Index Operations
nodes.description: Implements BPTreeIndex: NewPage / DeletePage (free-list management), InitializeIndex (header setup, max-size calculation), FindLeafPage / FindLeafPageForRange, Insert (leaf splits, parent propagation, root promotion), Delete (underflow resolution via redistribution or merge), Search, and SearchRange. Uses FetchPageRead / FetchPageWrite on the buffer pool manager and operates on the page structures above.

nodes.label: Index Handle
nodes.description: Wraps a concrete index (BPTreeIndex or HashIndex) behind a uniform interface: InsertRecord, DeleteRecord, UpdateRecord, SearchRange, CheckRecordExists. Delegates all operations to the index selected at construction time.

nodes.label: Index Scan Executor
nodes.description: Executes index-range scans: uses IndexHandle to obtain matching RIDs from the B+Tree, then fetches the actual records from TableHandle and applies filter conditions before emitting result tuples.

edges.label: BPM instantiates LRUReplacer when REPLACER config is 'LRUReplacer'
BPM instantiates LRUKReplacer when REPLACER config is 'LRUKReplacer'
PageGuard calls BPM::UnpinPage in destructor/Drop; BPM::FetchPageRead/Write return guard objects
TableHandle calls BPM FetchPage / UnpinPage / FetchPageWrite for all page I/O
TableHandle creates NAryPageHandle or PAXPageHandle to wrap fetched pages for slot access
BPTreeInternalPage::MoveHalfTo and CopyNFrom receive a BufferPoolManager* and call FetchPageWrite
BPTreeInternalPage structural helpers rely on WritePageGuard RAII (returned by FetchPageWrite) to update child parent pointers
BPTreeIndex reinterpret-casts page data to BPTreeLeafPage/BPTreeInternalPage for all tree navigation and restructuring
IndexHandle constructs a BPTreeIndex and delegates Insert/Delete/Search to it
IdxScanExecutor holds an IndexHandle* and calls SearchRange to obtain matching RIDs
IdxScanExecutor uses TableHandle* to fetch full records by the RIDs returned from the index
